name: Quantum Lane AI — Cyber Risk Assessment Ontology Service
description: Ontology-driven cyber risk assessment capability delivered as a consulting service for client prospecting, engagement deepening, and retention. Leverages graph-native analytics in Power Platform OneLake environments.

@graph.name: Ontology-Native Cyber Risk Intelligence Leader
@graph.description: Establish Quantum Lane AI as the definitive provider of ontology-driven cyber risk assessment services, transforming client security posture from reactive compliance checklists to proactive, graph-powered risk intelligence that integrates seamlessly into enterprise analytics ecosystems. Clients gain sustainable risk visibility through interconnected threat, asset, control, and compliance graphs rather than static spreadsheet-based assessments.

vsem:competitivePositioning.vsem:basis: Graph-native ontology architecture enabling contextual risk inference, causal chain analysis, and automated compliance mapping — capabilities impossible with flat assessment tools
vsem:competitivePositioning.vsem:targetMarket: Mid-market to enterprise organisations in regulated sectors (financial services, healthcare, critical infrastructure, UK insurers under PRA SS1/21)
vsem:competitivePositioning.vsem:valueProposition: Move from periodic audit snapshots to continuous, ontology-driven cyber risk intelligence that surfaces hidden dependencies, quantifies cascading failure impact, and auto-maps to multiple compliance frameworks simultaneously

@graph.name: Graph-Native Risk Ontology Development
@graph.description: Build and maintain a comprehensive cyber risk ontology grounded in Schema.org, NIST CSF 2.0, ISO 27001/27002:2022, and MITRE ATT&CK that models threats, vulnerabilities, assets, controls, compliance obligations, and business impact as an interconnected knowledge graph. Prototype in JSON-LD, deploy to Neo4j, surface through Power BI via OneLake DirectLake.

@graph.name: Client Prospecting via Risk Posture Benchmarking
@graph.description: Use the cyber risk ontology to generate sector-specific risk posture benchmarks that serve as lead magnets. Prospects receive a complimentary risk profile comparison against anonymised peer data from the graph, demonstrating immediate value and creating urgency for deeper engagement.

@graph.name: Engagement Deepening Through Continuous Risk Graph
@graph.description: Convert initial assessments into ongoing relationships by deploying persistent client risk graphs in their OneLake/Fabric environment. The graph continuously ingests telemetry, maps emerging threats via MITRE ATT&CK ontology edges, and surfaces actionable intelligence through Power BI dashboards — creating switching costs and demonstrating continuous value.

@graph.name: Retention via Regulatory Intelligence & Compliance Automation
@graph.description: Lock-in clients through automated compliance mapping that dynamically links their control implementations to evolving regulatory requirements (PRA SS1/21, DORA, NIS2, SOC2, ISO 27001). As regulations change, the ontology auto-identifies gaps — generating continuous advisory engagements and renewal justification.

@graph.name: Generate £500K recurring revenue from cyber risk ontology services within 18 months
@graph.description: Achieve £500K ARR from ontology-driven cyber risk assessment engagements including initial assessments (£15-50K), continuous graph deployments (£5-15K/month), and compliance automation subscriptions (£3-8K/month).

@graph.name: Achieve 70% gross margin on ontology-as-a-service engagements
@graph.description: Maintain 70%+ gross margin through ontology reuse, automated graph deployment pipelines, and template-driven compliance mapping that reduces per-client customisation effort.

@graph.name: Onboard 25 clients onto continuous risk graph within 24 months
@graph.description: Convert 25 organisations from initial assessment to persistent risk graph deployments in their Power Platform/OneLake environments, creating deep integration and switching costs.

@graph.name: Achieve 90%+ client retention rate on annual engagements
@graph.description: Retain 90% of clients year-over-year through continuous compliance intelligence updates, proactive threat landscape briefings generated from the ontology, and embedded Power BI dashboard stickiness.

@graph.name: Generate 100 qualified prospects via risk posture benchmarking within 12 months
@graph.description: Deliver complimentary sector-specific risk posture benchmarks to 100 qualified prospects, converting at least 25% to paid initial assessments.

@graph.name: Deploy production cyber risk ontology covering NIST CSF 2.0, ISO 27001, MITRE ATT&CK
@graph.description: Deliver a production-grade JSON-LD ontology with 500+ class instances covering threat actors, techniques, vulnerabilities, assets, controls, and compliance obligations with full cross-framework mapping.

@graph.name: Automate graph deployment pipeline for OneLake/Neo4j in under 2 hours
@graph.description: Build CI/CD pipeline that provisions a client-specific risk graph instance, seeds with sector template, connects to OneLake, and generates initial Power BI dashboards within 2 hours of engagement kickoff.

@graph.name: Achieve automated compliance gap detection across 5 regulatory frameworks
@graph.description: Enable automatic identification of control gaps when regulatory requirements change across PRA SS1/21, DORA, NIS2, SOC2 Type II, and ISO 27001:2022 through ontology reasoning over the client risk graph.

@graph.name: Build ontology engineering and graph analytics competency across delivery team
@graph.description: Train 6 consultants in knowledge graph modelling (JSON-LD, Neo4j Cypher, SPARQL), cyber risk domain expertise (NIST CSF, ISO 27001, MITRE ATT&CK), and Power Platform integration patterns.

@graph.name: Establish reusable sector ontology templates for 4 regulated industries
@graph.description: Create pre-built cyber risk ontology templates for Financial Services (PRA/FCA), Healthcare (DSPT), Critical Infrastructure (NIS2), and Insurance (PRA SS1/21) that accelerate deployment from weeks to hours.

@graph.name: Ontology Architecture & Schema.org Grounding
@graph.description: Ability to design, build, and maintain JSON-LD ontologies grounded in Schema.org with domain-specific extensions for cyber risk, compliance, and threat intelligence.

@graph.name: Graph Database Engineering (Neo4j / Cosmos DB Gremlin)
@graph.description: Ability to transform JSON-LD ontologies into performant graph databases, write Cypher/Gremlin queries for risk inference, and maintain graph schemas across client instances.

@graph.name: Sector Risk Benchmarking & Lead Generation
@graph.description: Ability to generate anonymised sector risk posture benchmarks from aggregated graph data and deliver as compelling prospect-facing reports.

@graph.name: Client Risk Profiling & Intake Assessment
@graph.description: Structured methodology for rapid client risk profiling using ontology-driven questionnaires that auto-populate the initial risk graph.

@graph.name: OneLake / Fabric Graph Integration
@graph.description: Ability to deploy and maintain Neo4j graph instances that synchronise with Microsoft Fabric OneLake via shortcuts, enabling DirectLake Power BI consumption of graph-derived risk analytics.

@graph.name: Power BI Cyber Risk Dashboard Design
@graph.description: Ability to design and deploy Power BI dashboards that consume graph-derived datasets from OneLake, visualising risk posture, control coverage, threat landscape, and compliance status.

@graph.name: Regulatory Ontology Maintenance & Gap Reasoning
@graph.description: Ability to model regulatory requirements as ontology entities, link to client controls, and run graph traversals to identify compliance gaps when regulations change.

@graph.name: AI-Augmented Advisory Report Generation
@graph.description: Ability to generate contextualised advisory reports using AI agents that traverse the client risk graph, identify material findings, and produce actionable recommendations grounded in ontology evidence.

@graph.name: Cyber Risk Service ARR
@graph.description: Annual recurring revenue from all cyber risk ontology service engagements

@graph.name: Ontology Service Gross Margin
@graph.description: Gross margin percentage on ontology-as-a-service engagements measuring reuse efficiency

@graph.name: Active Continuous Risk Graph Clients
@graph.description: Number of clients with deployed persistent risk graphs in their environment

@graph.name: Annual Client Retention Rate
@graph.description: Percentage of clients renewing annual risk graph and compliance subscriptions

@graph.name: Qualified Prospects via Benchmarking
@graph.description: Number of prospects engaged through complimentary risk posture benchmark reports

@graph.name: Ontology Class Coverage
@graph.description: Number of ontology class instances across threat, asset, control, and compliance domains

@graph.name: Graph Deployment Time
@graph.description: Time from engagement kickoff to operational client risk graph with initial dashboards

@graph.name: Regulatory Frameworks with Automated Gap Detection
@graph.description: Number of regulatory frameworks with fully modelled ontology entities enabling automated compliance gap identification

@graph.name: Certified Ontology/Graph Consultants
@graph.description: Number of consultants trained and certified in knowledge graph modelling and cyber risk domain expertise

@graph.name: Sector Ontology Templates Completed
@graph.description: Number of regulated industry verticals with production-ready cyber risk ontology templates
